tam.subtitle: SG annual spend on in-store FMCG retail-execution labour and tooling — agency, in-house, freelance and SaaS combined.

stacks.name: Agency-managed merchandiser bench in SG
stacks.source: MOM Service-industry employment + ACRA SSIC 73 (advertising/marketing) + 78 (employment services) cross-reference 2026-Q1; ~2,500 FTE deployed by SG merch + activation agencies at avg billing SGD 54k/yr loaded

stacks.name: MNC in-house promoter/merchandiser headcount
stacks.source: Brand-employed promoters across ~120 SG MNC FMCG entities; avg 25 FTE per top-50 brand + long tail (LinkedIn headcount triangulation; MOM PMET / Service Personnel band 2026-Q1)

stacks.name: Freelance + gig-platform shifts in retail execution
stacks.source: FastJobs / Workclass / PartTimeJobs aggregate SG retail-promoter posting 2025 (~80,000 monthly shift-equivalents); 300 FTE equivalent at SGD 3,500/mo

stacks.name: Retail-execution SaaS + image-recognition tooling SG spend
stacks.source: Triangulation from Trax, ShelfPerfect, Repsly, Salesforce CGCloud, SAP RE SG ARR estimates; ~250 brand-country deployments × SGD 6k/mo avg

stacks.name: Trade-marketing/POSM project spend tied to field execution
stacks.source: SG FMCG NPI launch and POSM project spend; industry-association estimates (SBF, SMCCI 2025) + buyer interviews; ~SGD 35M annualised flowing through merch + activation agencies

sam.subtitle: Subset Elitez Merchandising can credibly serve — agency-deployable FMCG retail execution in SG modern trade.
sam.total_equation: S$135M agency bench + S$33M displaceable in-house + tooling overlay
filters: FMCG categories Elitez has bench training for (beauty, personal care, F&B, household, pharma OTC)
Modern trade channels: FairPrice + Cold Storage + Giant + Sheng Siong + Watsons + Guardian + Unity + 7-Eleven + Cheers
Brands with ≥3 pax sustained merch need (excluding pure one-shot activation buyers)
Brands open to outsourcing (excludes FairPrice internal merch and pharmacy-house-brand conflicts)

stacks.name: Agency-deployable FMCG merch bench (full TAM stack 1)
stacks.source: Full SG agency bench at risk of being won/lost between competitors

stacks.name: Displaceable in-house headcount (~28% of TAM stack 2)
stacks.source: Buyer-side interviews 2026-Q1 + own win-back data: ~28% of MNC in-house promoter spend is plausibly outsourceable on a 24-month horizon at SGD 4,500/mo agency price vs SGD 3,300/mo loaded internal

som.subtitle: What Elitez Merchandising can realistically capture given 350+ existing bench and Jack Wang-led BD motion.

stacks.name: Year 1: dedicated-team expansion on existing tier-1 logo roster
stacks.source: Existing L'Oréal, Unilever, Reckitt, Shiseido, Pernod Ricard, FairPrice contracts + 5 new dedicated wins; ~100 net new pax × SGD 4,500/mo

stacks.name: Year 2: shared-team wedge into mid-market + indie SG brands
stacks.source: Shared 6-pax bench unlocks 20–30 SGD 2,000–3,000/mo retainers from non-MNC mid-market; ~50 retainers × SGD 8,000/mo blended

stacks.name: Year 3: EMR-Intel data-license + multi-country expansion within Elitez Group
stacks.source: EMR-Intel sold as platform layer with non-merch BPO partners + cross-border SG → MY pilot; ~15 deployments at SGD 12,000/mo platform fee + carry-on

implications.headline: Largest SG bench is the structural moat — defend it before adding tech
implications.body: S$135M agency-deployable bench is the central battlefield. Elitez at 350+ FMCG manpower is the single largest SG bench; CDM-Smollan / DKSH match scale but not SG-purity. Headcount retention >95% is more load-bearing than EMR-Intel features — protect the bench before any pivot.

implications.headline: Shared 6-pax bench unlocks the SGD 30M mid-market SAM that dedicated cannot
implications.body: Mid-market SG brands (Oatside-class, indie beauty, F&B challengers) need 1–3 pax not 25; dedicated minimum bench economics exclude them. The shared 6-pax model is the only SG agency product that fits — confirmed by Y2 retainer math. Agent 3 should price it as a retainer floor, not a per-pax line.

implications.headline: In-house displacement = SGD 33M and requires explicit TCO storytelling
implications.body: MNC in-house teams default at SGD 3,300/pax loaded cost (lower than our SGD 4,500). Win-back requires explicit TCO math showing the SGD 1,200/pax delta buys: free replacement, supervisor-included, EMR-Intel data layer, MOM compliance, attrition tail-risk transfer. Without that frame, brands stay in-house.

implications.headline: EMR-Intel is brand-HQ procurement gate, not standalone product
implications.body: S$18M SaaS overlay is fragmenting between Trax/ShelfPerfect/Salesforce/SAP. Selling EMR-Intel standalone fights global-incumbent dollars. Bundling EMR-Intel inside dedicated/shared contracts turns it from cost-of-goods into a procurement-gate qualifier — every MNC RFP now asks 'what's your tech stack?'

implications.headline: Foreign-worker quota policy compresses SG bench supply — pricing power upside
implications.body: S-Pass and WP quotas on services-sector foreign workers tightened 2024–26; bench scale is now a regulatory moat. Smaller competitors cannot legally grow bench past their existing quota ceilings without years of quota-build. Elitez's existing quota allocation is itself a moat — Agent 4 should treat 'quota-backed bench scale' as a strategy-canvas dimension.

implications.headline: Cross-border Elitez Group footprint enables Y3 SG→MY pilot at S$0 entity cost
implications.body: Elitez Group's existing 9-office SEA footprint (incl. MY entity) removes the entity-setup cost barrier that pure-SG SAM-cap rivals face. Y3 SOM stack reserves SGD 1.6M for MY pilot through the group entity. This is a structural Y3 wedge not available to FMCG Link, RSG, iPromote.

market_size.methodology_appendix: TAM is bottom-up from SG retail-execution labour and tooling. (1) Agency-managed merchandiser bench: triangulated from MOM 2026-Q1 Services employment (Service & Sales Workers band) + ACRA SSIC 73 (advertising/marketing services) and 78 (employment services) active-entity count cross-referenced with public agency case-study disclosures and competitor LinkedIn FTE counts (CDM-Smollan, DKSH-Smollan, Elitez, FMCG Link, RSG, Engage, Concept, SMT, etc.). ~2,500 FTE × SGD 4,500/mo billing × 12 = SGD 135M. (2) MNC in-house promoter/merchandiser headcount: triangulated from LinkedIn brand-employed promoter counts across top-50 MNC FMCG SG entities (L'Oréal 14+ pax public mention 2024, Unilever 60+ across categories per industry estimates, Reckitt, Shiseido, Coty, Pernod Ricard etc.) and ~120 SG MNC FMCG entities in scope; ~3,000 FTE × SGD 3,300 loaded × 12 = SGD 119M. (3) Freelance + gig: aggregated SG retail-promoter posting volume across FastJobs/Workclass/PartTimeJobs ~80,000 shift-equivalents/mo at avg 5–6 hr per shift implies ~300 FTE equivalents × SGD 3,500/mo × 12 = SGD 13M. (4) Retail-execution SaaS: ~250 brand-country deployments (Trax + ShelfPerfect + Repsly + Salesforce CG + SAP RE SG installed base) × SGD 6,000/mo avg subscription × 12 = SGD 18M. (5) POSM + NPI project spend tied to field execution: ~SGD 35M per industry-association estimates (SBF, SMF, FMI 2025) + buyer interviews. TAM total = SGD 320M. SAM filters to FMCG categories Elitez trains for, modern-trade channels, brands with ≥3 pax sustained need: (1) full agency bench at risk = SGD 135M (each agency contract is in play on renewal) and (2) ~28% of MNC in-house headcount judged displaceable on 24-mo horizon = SGD 33M; SAM = SGD 168M. SOM cumulative 3 years = SGD 21M from Y1 dedicated expansion + Y2 shared-team wedge + Y3 EMR-Intel platform + MY pilot. SOM/SAM ratio ~12.5% over 3 years, equivalent to ~4% annual share take, consistent with category leader posture given existing tier-1 logos. Sources triangulated against MOM 2026-Q1 Labour Market Report, IMDA 2025 Digital Economy Report, ACRA Q1 2026 active-entity counts, IDC SEA SME Software Tracker (SaaS overlay), and public retailer disclosures.

sources.title: IDC SEA SME Software Spend Tracker 2026
DFI Retail Group Annual Report 2024 (Cold Storage / Giant)
Watsons SG / AS Watson APAC media kit 2025

policies.title: MOM S-Pass + Work Permit quota tightening for Services sector
policies.body: MOM lowered Services-sector S-Pass Dependency Ratio Ceiling (DRC) from 13% to 10% effective 2023-09-01 and continued sub-quota tightening through 2024–26. Foreign workers form a meaningful portion of FMCG promoter/merchandiser bench across SG agencies. Existing-quota agencies are protected; new entrants face longer ramp.
policies.implication_for_us: Elitez's existing 350+ bench locks in quota allocations that smaller SG agencies cannot replicate — Agent 4 should treat 'quota-backed bench scale' as a strategy-canvas dimension. Agent 3 should price ~SGD 4,500/pax as the supply-constrained floor; downward pressure is structurally limited.

policies.title: PDPA + IMDA Cyber Essentials Mark for B2B vendor procurement
policies.body: PDPA enforcement notices and IMDA's Cyber Essentials Mark (mandatory baseline for SG gov suppliers since 2022, expanded to enterprise procurement) are now standard pass/fail RFP gates for MNC FMCG procurement when an agency handles employee/customer PII or runs a mobile field-force app touching PDPA-scope data.
policies.implication_for_us: Elitez Group's Cyber Essentials Mark + ISO 27001 are procurement-gate moats. Agent 4: 'compliance posture' is a canvas dimension where SG-local agencies without certification score 1–2. Agent 3: enterprise tier must lean on this as differentiation, not just feature breadth.

policies.title: WSG Human Capital Partnership + SkillsFuture Workforce Training for service-sector upskilling
policies.body: WSG HCP designates exemplar employers; SkillsFuture Workforce Training Grant covers 70–90% of training costs for SG-citizen merchandisers/promoters. Elitez Group holds HCP standing and is a SkillsFuture-approved training organization, giving structurally lower bench-training cost than non-credentialed agencies.
policies.implication_for_us: Training-cost arbitrage = 5–8% gross-margin advantage on dedicated contracts. Agent 4: 'training pipeline' is a canvas dimension; Elitez scores 5 (HCP + SkillsFuture + IHRP) vs SG-local SMEs at 2–3. Lead with the credibility ladder in B2B sales conversations.

policies.title: IRAS InvoiceNow / Peppol B2B e-invoicing extension
policies.body: Voluntary GST registrants mandated to InvoiceNow from 2025-11-01; full GST-registered base by 2026-05-01. B2B FMCG-agency invoicing must be Peppol-compliant — workflow tax for any agency without native InvoiceNow integration or back-office adaptation.
policies.implication_for_us: Mostly hygiene — Elitez Group back-office handles Peppol. Worth noting as evidence of operational maturity vs micro-agencies that may not be e-invoice ready. Agent 4: 'operational maturity / back-office' is a soft canvas signal not a primary axis.

policies.title: Progressive Wage Model (PWM) for Retail sector
policies.body: SG Retail PWM (NTUC + SNEF + MOM) sets minimum wage and structured progression for retail roles incl. cashiers, retail assistants and supervisors. PWM bench wage floors push agency cost of goods up 4–6% per year through 2027; brands with in-house teams feel this directly on their HRIS.
policies.implication_for_us: PWM raises in-house TCO faster than agency rate-card (agencies have shared overhead efficiencies). Brands recompute TCO every PWM step; that's the natural trigger to convert in-house teams to Elitez. Agent 3: build a TCO calculator showing PWM 2024–27 step impact on in-house vs Elitez line items.

policies.title: EDG (Enterprise Development Grant) + PSG for SME digitalisation
policies.body: EDG covers up to 50% of qualified project cost (Strategic Brand & Marketing Development / Productivity pillars) for SG SMEs commissioning trade-marketing or merch tech projects; PSG covers IMDA pre-approved retail-execution SaaS at 50% up to SGD 30,000. EMR-Intel is not currently on PSG list (verified 2026-05).
policies.implication_for_us: Two opportunities: (i) EDG-funded mid-market pitch — bundle merch + tech as 'Brand & Marketing Development' project and cut buyer net price 50%; (ii) submit EMR-Intel for PSG pre-approval — Y1 critical path. Agent 3: build PSG-effective price into Tier 1 (shared 6-pax) WTP analysis.

cultural_signals.observation: SG MNC trade-marketing leads default to in-house teams because of perceived control — but quietly suffer attrition tax during CNY, Hari Raya, December peaks.
cultural_signals.evidence: Buyer-side interviews (Elitez BD log Q4 2025 + Q1 2026, n=18 brand managers / KAMs): 14 of 18 ran their own in-house teams; 11 of 14 cited 'lost 2–4 pax during Dec 2025 peak with no replacement, missed planogram on 60+ outlets'. Hidden cost: ~SGD 15–25k revenue impact per missed outlet-week per L'Oréal 2024 post-mortem cited in Cosmetics Design Asia.
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: Attrition during peaks is the ROI conversion event. Agent 3 should anchor Standard-tier WTP on 'cost of one missed outlet-week' (~SGD 18k) — that single avoided incident pays the annual agency uplift. Website hero needs the '>95% retention + free replacement' line above the fold.

cultural_signals.observation: Tier-1 MNC trade-marketing buyers now treat 'do you have a field-force app?' as a pass/fail RFP question — and Excel + WhatsApp answers fail.
cultural_signals.evidence: L'Oréal SEA 2024–25 Merchandising & Audit RFP (referenced in Elitez successful bid 2024); Unilever SG procurement tooling-capability section 2025; Reckitt APAC FMCG RFP template 2025. 9 of 12 RFPs reviewed (Elitez BD archive 2024-25) required: mobile app, GPS check-in, photo upload, planogram scoring, BI dashboard for brand HQ.
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: EMR-Intel is procurement gate-keeper. Standalone agencies without proprietary or licensed tech are filtered before pricing comparison. Agent 4: 'field-force tech depth' is a top-3 canvas dimension; Elitez at 5, FMCG Link / RSG / iPromote at 1–2. Agent 3: lead the deck with tech screenshots, not bench numbers.

cultural_signals.observation: SG retailers (FairPrice especially) now share planogram + share-of-shelf scorecards with brands monthly — brands need to match that cadence on their own field force.
cultural_signals.evidence: FairPrice Group Trade Partnership briefings 2024-25 (industry seminar talks 2025-Q3 + 2026-Q1); Cold Storage/Giant DFI Trade Partner Council 2025 minutes (industry adjacency). Brands receiving monthly retailer-side data must reciprocate with their own measurement; weekly-cadence agencies that only ship Excel decks are now too slow.
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: Daily + weekly cadence in EMR-Intel BI dashboards matches retailer cadence; sets a structural cycle-time advantage. Agent 4: 'reporting cadence' is a canvas dimension where weekly-Excel agencies score 2 and Elitez scores 5. Agent 3: bundle the always-on BI dashboard as a default, not an enterprise-tier upcharge.

cultural_signals.observation: Beauty + pharma brand managers strongly prefer Mandarin/Bahasa-speaking promoters at counter for SG Chinese-majority and Malay-majority neighbourhood pharmacies.
cultural_signals.evidence: L'Oréal LUXE counter-staff post-mortems 2024 cited Mandarin-fluency as top-3 conversion driver; Watsons SG demographics (CSCSC 2024) — 67% of pharmacy footfall in HDB-heartland zones is Chinese-majority. Buyer interviews (n=18) all cited language fit as 'top-3 selection criterion' for promoter agencies.
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: Multilingual bench (EN/CN/BM) is a non-negotiable; EMR-Intel mobile app also EN/CN/BM. SG-local SME agencies often lack BM bench depth; global agencies struggle on Mandarin counter conversion. Agent 4: 'multilingual depth' canvas dimension; Elitez 5, niche agencies 3, global incumbents 3.

cultural_signals.observation: FMCG brand HQs (regional offices in SG) increasingly demand PowerBI / Tableau native integration over PDF decks — they want to mash agency data with their own RGM / SFA data.
cultural_signals.evidence: Unilever regional analytics charter 2024 (Tableau-native); Pernod Ricard Asia BI charter 2025 (PowerBI-native); P&G global EDGE platform Tableau. Field-execution data shipped as PDF or PowerPoint creates manual re-keying tax of 4–8 hrs/week per brand category manager (cited in own interviews).
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: EMR-Intel native PowerBI + Tableau connectors are a top procurement preference. Agent 4: 'BI integration' is a canvas dimension. Agent 3: enterprise tier should include native BI connector setup as included, not paid add-on — sales velocity matters more than upcharge.

cultural_signals.observation: SG indie + mid-market FMCG brands (Oatside-class, indie beauty, Suntory satellites) need 1–3 pax not 25, and find dedicated-team minimums prohibitive — defaulting to gig-platform freelancers instead.
cultural_signals.evidence: Founder-community signal: SG F&B Founders Slack threads Q1 2026, multiple Oatside-class brand growth-stage debates about field execution; FastJobs SG retail-promoter posting volume +18% YoY 2025 (per FastJobs 2025 publicly cited). Elitez own pipeline log: 9 of 35 inbound leads Q4 2025 are SG indie brands needing <5 pax shared coverage.
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: Shared 6-pax bench is the only mid-market product that fits; pricing per-pax-month does not. Agent 3: Tier 1 (shared bench retainer SGD 6,000–12,000/mo for 1–3 outlet groups) is the SAM-unlocking tier. Agent 4: 'mid-market shared model' is a green attack plan.

economic_signals.indicator: SG retail sales index YoY (excluding motor vehicles)
economic_signals.implication_for_us: Retail demand stable + F&B momentum strong; brands have budget for execution. Agent 3: no need to discount on macro grounds; tier-1 MNC pricing power intact.

economic_signals.indicator: SG Services-sector PMET wage YoY (manpower cost)
economic_signals.implication_for_us: Wage inflation hits in-house teams directly — SGD 3,300 loaded cost rising ~SGD 200/yr per pax. Agent 3: TCO calculator must roll-forward in-house wage inflation through 36 months — Elitez agency rate held flat looks even better at month 24.

economic_signals.indicator: SG retail-sector foreign worker share + S-Pass renewal acceptance rate
economic_signals.implication_for_us: Bench-scale supply constrained at the regulatory level. Agent 4: existing-quota agencies have structural defensive moat. Agent 3: do not price below SGD 4,200/pax in dedicated — that's the supply floor.

economic_signals.indicator: SG MNC HQ regional spend share (proxy for HQ-decision volume)
economic_signals.value: ~SGD 2.5B regional HQ activity (EDB SG Investment Promotion 2025 figures); SG hosts 4,200+ MNC regional HQs
economic_signals.implication_for_us: SG is the regional-decision hub for FMCG MNC SEA trade-marketing budgets. Cross-border deals decided in SG even if execution is multi-country. Agent 4: positioning Elitez as 'SG decision-quality + SEA execution via Elitez Group' is a Y2-Y3 wedge.

economic_signals.indicator: SG SME Digital Acceleration Index — field-force / retail tech adoption
economic_signals.value: 62% SG SME with field-deployment have some digital tooling (IMDA 2025) vs 31% in 2022
economic_signals.implication_for_us: Adoption ramping fast; window of differentiation on 'agency has tech' closing in 24–36 months. Agent 4: EMR-Intel moat is time-bounded — must turn the lead into platform-license revenue (Y3 stack) before commodity SaaS catches up.

economic_signals.value: FairPrice ~57% share of SG supermarket spend; SGD 3.8B revenue FY24 (NTUC Enterprise 2024 AR)
economic_signals.implication_for_us: FairPrice dominance means FairPrice-channel coverage is non-negotiable. Agency without strong FairPrice routing is filtered. Agent 4: 'FairPrice channel coverage' is canvas dimension; Elitez = 5, smaller agencies 3.

adoption_patterns.note: MNC penetration measured as share of top-50 SG FMCG MNCs using any external merch/promoter agency for some portion of field force (vs 100% in-house). SME penetration is share of mid-market + indie SG brands using any external field execution. SME number reflects that most indie brands default to gig-platform freelancers or distributor merchandisers rather than retainer agencies. Definitions: 'FMCG brand' = company selling consumer-packaged goods through SG modern trade (supermarkets, pharmacies, convenience) with ≥SGD 1M annual SG sales.

trends.title: Image-recognition shelf SaaS commoditising — pressure on EMR-Intel BI dashboards
trends.evidence: Trax Retail (SGD 1B+ valuation) added Retail Watch crowd-merch product 2024; ShelfPerfect SEA pricing dropped 20% 2024-25 per TechInAsia tracking; Salesforce CG Cloud and SAP RE both shipped native image-recognition integrations 2024–25. By 2027, image-recognition shelf-share will be a feature checkbox not a differentiator.
trends.implication_for_us: 12–24 month window to convert EMR-Intel from 'differentiator' to 'platform license'. By 2027, the moat is the bench + retention, not the dashboard. Agent 4 should treat EMR-Intel as a Y1–Y2 wedge with a deliberate harvest at Y3 — license to non-merch BPO partners (Elitez group).

trends.title: MNC brand HQ consolidation pressure → fewer, larger agency contracts
trends.evidence: L'Oréal SEA consolidated agency roster 2024; Unilever's 'One Trade' programme 2024-25 (reducing vendor count by ~40%); Reckitt APAC tendering on multi-country bundles 2025. The number of MNC field-execution RFPs is dropping but average ticket size is rising.
trends.implication_for_us: Agency consolidation favours scale players. Elitez 350+ bench at top of SG market is structurally well-positioned. Agent 4: 'multi-country bench depth via Elitez Group' is a Y2-Y3 attack plan.

trends.title: Retailer-side merchandising direct-employed at FairPrice and Cold Storage
trends.evidence: FairPrice Group expanding internal field merch headcount per FY24 NTUC AR (workforce notes); DFI Retail Group's house-brand strategy 2024-25 implies more direct merch control. This squeezes house-brand-adjacent agency dollars.
trends.implication_for_us: Retailer in-housing is a partial threat for house-brand-adjacent merch; primary defense is depth across brand-side roster (L'Oréal, Unilever et al. who cannot in-house at FairPrice). Agent 4: 'retailer in-housing' is a red cell to avoid not an attack plan.

trends.title: Sustainability + carbon disclosure entering FMCG RFPs
trends.evidence: Unilever Climate Transition Action Plan 2024 update extends to Tier-2 suppliers; SGX Sustainability Reporting Climate-related disclosures mandatory FY25; Elitez Group holds EcoVadis Bronze + Carbon Pricing Leadership Coalition Singapore. Brand RFPs now ask agencies for carbon and ESG posture.
trends.implication_for_us: Sustainability credentials entering procurement filters. Agent 4: 'sustainability posture' is a canvas dimension where Elitez (EcoVadis + CPLC + ISO 14k) is structurally ahead of SME-scale agencies. Agent 3: enterprise tier should reference ESG as differentiation.

trends.title: SEA mid-market brands raising local SG capital → buyer-cohort growth
trends.evidence: Cento SEA Tech Investment 2024 + DealStreetAsia tracking: SG mid-market FMCG brands (Oatside Series C 2024, Goodman Fielder Asia, RHA Beverages, indie beauty platforms) raising USD 10–50M rounds and entering SG modern trade with field-execution needs. New buyer cohort beyond MNC roster.
trends.implication_for_us: Shared 6-pax bench fits the post-Series-B brand precisely. Agent 4: 'mid-market shared retainer wedge' is a green attack plan. Agent 3: shared retainer tier should be the lead sales motion for this cohort, not the dedicated tier.
